description: List. Collection, jibaya, made on the first day of the holiday for the Maghribi, the Kohen. About 1115 contributions, mostly in Coptic numerals. Some of the names are encircled (represented here by $ $, perhaps indicating persons solicited). Other names are crossed through (= having paid?). A few are left untouched. The list is headed by the haver ʿOvadya, presumably the great grandson of Avraham Maimonides bearing that name (b. 1297, d. before 1355). The other names also fit the 14th century, e.g Nasir ("helper," three times), Shams ("sun," 11. 4, 27), Faraj Allah ("God has helped," 11. 8, 14). Three dashes (---) represent a diagonal slash downward toward the left, which stands for 1/2 elsewhere but here must mean something else, since 1/2 in this list is representeed by the left arrow, as elsewhere. (S/D. Goitein, Mediterranean Society, 2:496, App. C. 68) EMS
tags: charity, poverty, communal, Coptic numerals, donors
description_he: גבייה שנערכה ביום הראשון של החג למגרבי הכהן. כ-1115 תרומות בעיקר בספרות קופטיות. הרשימה בראשה החבר עובדיה, ככל הנראה נינו של אברהם מיימוניד. שמות אחרים מאפיינים את המאה ה-14.
